items.rationale: Core thesis: crypto/web3 is the only credible path to restore the internet's original vision as an open platform where network effects accrue as public goods and power flows to the edges, not incumbents.
items.age_note: Continuously held from Jan 2022 through the June 2023 book announcement; most recent framing is the strongest and most synthesized.

citations.quote: crypto/web3 is the only plausible path to sustaining the original vision of the internet as an open platform that incentivizes creativity & entrepreneurship.
web3 provides a new way to build networks that combines the publicly-owned network effects of web1 with the advanced functionality of web2.

items.rationale: Sees community-driven, open-economy games (player agency, composability, open economies) as a frontier; backed a $40M AAA EVE Universe investment in CCP.

citations.quote: We believe @CCPGames will help shape the next generation of web3 games... enable a new frontier in player agency and autonomy.
@a16z is leading a $40M investment in @CCPGames' new AAA gaming project set in the EVE Universe.

items.rationale: NFTs add a new layer of value—digital ownership—and let creators go direct to audiences capturing 90%+ of revenue versus extractive web2 take rates.
items.age_note: Articulated most intensively in Jan–Mar 2022; not restated as standalone topic recently but fully consistent with and subsumed by the ongoing web3 thesis.

citations.quote: NFTs add a new layer of value — digital ownership — that didn't exist in a credible way before NFTs.
In web3, creators can go direct to their audiences by selling things like NFTs, earning 90% or more of the revenue they generate.

items.rationale: Web2 take rates (30-100%) versus web3's ~2.5%; data portability lowers switching costs, making web3 especially appealing to creators underserved by web2.
items.age_note: Stated Jan–Mar 2022, not recently restated but a durable structural argument.

citations.quote: web2's take rate is web3's opportunity
OpenSea, for example, charges 2.5% on transactions... YouTube takes 45% of revenue. Apple and Google app stores take 30%.

items.rationale: With both Meta and Apple investing, VR will follow prior computing-platform cost/performance curves and become cheaper, faster, lighter—a non-crypto personal enthusiasm.

citations.quote: Excited for Vision Pro. With Meta and now Apple investment, VR will follow the same pattern as past computing platforms.

items.rationale: Tokens are self-marketing and overcome the cold-start bootstrap problem; cited Helium's token-incentivized grassroots telecom network as the proof case.

citations.quote: web3 enables projects to offer token incentives to users in the early phase when the native network effects are weak.
in web3 tokens are self-marketing. When someone genuinely owns something and feels skin in the game, they want to evangelize it.

items.rationale: Novel constructs that supercharge online communities with sophisticated coordination and native financial resources; sees an underexplored design space.
items.age_note: Stated Mar 2022 only; aging and never restated, so treated as persisted lower-conviction view.

citations.quote: DAOs are novel web3 constructs that supercharge online communities by providing more sophisticated coordination methods.
we are still early in the exploration of the DAO design space. There will likely be opportunities to apply DAOs to novel areas.

items.rationale: Sees the UK's sandbox, outcomes-based, consumer-protection-forward approach as the right path; opening a London office and moving Crypto Startup School there.

citations.quote: The UK is on the right path for crypto regulation... Innovative sandbox approach to regulation... Consumer protection front-and-center
Big news to share: @a16z is expanding to the UK

items.rationale: Views the speculative/gambling culture as harmful to the space and explicitly wants it stamped out; flags it as a controversial topic he confronts directly.

citations.quote: stamping out the harmful "casino" culture that has developed around crypto.
I tackle controversial topics like policy/regulatory, and the harmful "casino" culture that has developed around crypto.

items.rationale: Wants the US to take a more constructive approach; praises Coinbase's compliance-despite-cost and pushes for regulatory clarity so startups can build a pathway onshore.
items.age_note: Consistent across Mar and Jun 2023; current and active concern.

citations.quote: We hope the US will take a more constructive approach to collaborating with innovators while protecting consumers.
Crypto/web3 is still in the early innings, and needs clear regulations to provide an open pathway for startups.

items.rationale: A few entrenched tech incumbents extract data/money and aggressively suppress threats, risking an internet like broadcast TV with no room for startups.
items.age_note: Most explicit in 2022; remains the durable motivating concern behind the whole web3 thesis (echoed implicitly in the Jun 2023 book framing).

citations.quote: There is a serious risk the internet turns out like, say, broadcast TV with a few dominant corporate-owned services, and no room for startups.
A handful of tech incumbents dominate the internet... Entrenched interests aggressively respond to new movements that might someday threaten them.

items.rationale: Acknowledges possibility of a prolonged 2008-style downturn and tactical need to preserve capital, but downplays it—urges ignoring financial-cycle noise and focusing on the product cycle.
items.age_note: From Jun 2022; framed as a managed/secondary concern rather than a deterrent, and not restated since.

citations.quote: If we are headed for an economic downturn, there are some tactical lessons from the 2008 era, namely preserving capital.
the main lesson is to ignore the noise and stay squarely focused on the product cycle.

items.rationale: Treated as the reference network where users and NFTs interoperate and users hold their own data; the substrate for his NFT and data-portability arguments.
items.age_note: Referenced as core platform in early 2022; cited as infrastructure rather than a price/token call.

citations.quote: Ethereum NFTs can be viewed as a network where users and NFTs interoperate and form connections... Users have full control of their data.

items.rationale: Held up as the flagship example of token incentives building a nationwide grassroots network to challenge telecom incumbents.
items.age_note: Single mention Mar 2022 as an illustrative case, not a recent or explicit token pick.

citations.quote: Helium, which has used token incentives to build a nationwide grassroots telecom service to take on the telecom incumbents.

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish: believes crypto/web3 will be at the center of the next cycle and is in 'early innings,' and prioritizes the product cycle over financial-cycle noise. Actively expanding (UK office, new gaming investment, book launch) as of mid-2023. Risk-on posture is tempered—not reversed—by concern over crypto's 'casino' culture and demand for regulatory clarity, which he frames as cleanup needed for durable long-term building rather than reasons to pull back.

notes: View is overwhelmingly thematic/structural (open internet, ownership, creator economics) rather than token-specific—he names projects as illustrations, not investment picks, so token sections are thin and low-confidence by design. Most recent corpus (Mar–Jun 2023) shows continuity, not reversal: the 2022 NFT/take-rate/DAO arguments persist and are consolidated into the June 2023 'Read Write Own' book framing. No stance reversals detected. Notable evolution: a sharper, more self-critical tone on crypto's speculative 'casino' culture and a strong recent emphasis on constructive regulation (pro-UK, wary of US ambiguity)—a maturation of, not a break from, his long-run optimism. The 2008-analogy macro caution (Jun 2022) is the oldest concern and is explicitly subordinated to product-cycle focus.
